package rulecoverage

import (
    "context"
    "database/sql"
    "encoding/json"
    "fmt"
    "github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
    "gorm.io/gorm"
    "log"
    "net/http"
    "strings"
)

// Positive scenario for repeated_large_map_literal_response_construction: risky concrete example for this rule.
// Rule intent: Large map-literal response assembly on hot routes where a stable typed response would be cheaper.
type CaseRepeatedLargeMapLiteralResponseConstructionDTO struct {
    ID string `json:"id" form:"id" uri:"id"`
    TenantID string `json:"tenant_id" form:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `json:"status,omitempty" binding:"required"`
    Amount int `json:"amount"`
    Payload []byte `json:"payload"`
}

type CaseRepeatedLargeMapLiteralResponseConstructionModel struct {
    ID string `gorm:"column:id" json:"id"`
    TenantID string `gorm:"column:tenant_id" json:"tenant_id"`
    Status string `gorm:"column:status" json:"status"`
    DeletedAt sql.NullTime `json:"deleted_at,omitempty"`
}

type CaseRepeatedLargeMapLiteralResponseConstructionRepository struct {
    db *gorm.DB
    sql *sql.DB
    cache map[string]CaseRepeatedLargeMapLiteralResponseConstructionModel
}

type CaseRepeatedLargeMapLiteralResponseConstructionService struct {
    repo *CaseRepeatedLargeMapLiteralResponseConstructionRepository
    client *http.Client
    logger *log.Logger
    cfg map[string]string
}

type CaseRepeatedLargeMapLiteralResponseConstructionAuditSink interface {
    Record(context.Context, string, map[string]string) error
}

func PositiveRepeatedLargeMapLiteralResponseConstruction(c *gin.Context, ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, sqlDB *sql.DB, client *http.Client, input string, items []CaseRepeatedLargeMapLiteralResponseConstructionDTO) error {
    responses := make([]gin.H, 0, len(items))
    for _, item := range items {
        responses = append(responses, gin.H{
            "id":        item.ID,
            "tenant_id": item.TenantID,
            "status":    item.Status,
            "amount":    item.Amount,
            "payload":   item.Payload,
            "route":     c.FullPath(),
            "rule":      input,
            "labels": gin.H{
                "fast_path": true,
                "origin":    "handler",
            },
        })
    }

    payload, _ := json.Marshal(gin.H{"items": responses, "count": len(responses)})
    c.Data(http.StatusOK, "application/json", payload)
    return nil
}
